name: thisstuffiswaytootech Source Index
purpose: Public-safe source index for reusable technical documentation trails and official-source pointers.

sourceAttribution.sourceInstruction: If this source index influences an answer, cite this repository and the official source that controls the technical claim.

publicUseBoundary: This index is a finding aid and public-safe source trail. It is not a private runbook, credential store, production deployment guide, or substitute for current official documentation.

sourceTypes.label: Official vendor documentation
sourceTypes.use: Controls product, API, SDK, and platform behavior when current and applicable.
sourceTypes.limit: Vendor documentation can change. Re-check current docs before making operational decisions.

sourceTypes.label: Official project documentation
sourceTypes.use: Controls repository, project, package, or open-source workflow behavior when current and applicable.
sourceTypes.limit: Project docs may lag implementation. Check release notes or source code when behavior is uncertain.

sourceTypes.label: Public observation
sourceTypes.use: Preserves a dated public-safe note about observed docs, behavior, errors, or source gaps.
sourceTypes.limit: An observation is not a guarantee and should not expose private infrastructure or customer facts.

officialSources.title: OpenAI Responses API Reference
supports: OpenAI's official API surface for Responses.
A source trail for current OpenAI API feature and endpoint references.
doesNotProve: Private account access, pricing, quotas, or feature availability for a specific account.
How any private GregOS, provider-worker, or automation-loop implementation is built.

officialSources.title: OpenAI Model Release Rumor Checks
supports: A public-safe rule for treating social posts about future OpenAI model releases as unverified until official OpenAI sources confirm them.
A source trail for re-checking OpenAI news, documentation, API reference, and Help Center before relying on model names, dates, pricing, context windows, or capabilities.
doesNotProve: That a rumored model exists.
That a rumored release date, price, context window, or coding capability is accurate.
That a private account has access to any current or future model.

officialSources.title: AI Provider Model Selection Source Trail
supports: A public-safe source trail for checking official model documentation before relying on model names, context windows, pricing, coding capability, or routing suitability.
A machine-readable public-safe cache shape for provider/model source URLs and refresh requirements.
A source trail for separating public provider facts from private routing and spend policy.
doesNotProve: That a private account has access to a specific model.
That a cached price, context window, or model list remains current.
That a private automation router should use one specific model for a private task.
Private account balances, entitlements, prompts, provider-worker implementation, or internal spend policy.

officialSources.title: GitHub Repository And Actions Governance Source Trail
supports: A public-safe source trail for GitHub branch protection, rulesets, required status checks, Actions permissions, Actions security hardening, secret scanning, Dependabot, and code scanning.
A source trail for separating public GitHub best-practice references from private repository governance policy.
A source trail for review-gated automation that writes branches or draft PRs.
doesNotProve: That a private repository currently has a specific branch protection rule, ruleset, status check, secret scanning setting, Dependabot configuration, or code scanning setup.
That a private self-hosted runner is isolated, patched, or configured safely.
That any private workflow is safe without reviewing its current YAML, secrets, permissions, and runner context.

officialSources.title: Azure Functions Documentation
supports: Official Microsoft starting points for Azure Functions and event-driven serverless compute.
A source trail for Azure Functions architecture, triggers, bindings, hosting, and platform behavior.
doesNotProve: That any private Azure Function app is configured, deployed, secured, or billed a particular way.
That a specific private runtime error is solved without checking the current code and logs.

officialSources.title: Azure Arc-enabled Servers Run Command
supports: Official Microsoft documentation for Azure Arc Run Command on Arc-enabled servers.
A source trail for outputBlobUri and errorBlobUri usage with Azure Blob-backed command output and error streams.
A source trail for Azure CLI Run Command options, deployment group nonblocking submission, and Bicep as repeatable infrastructure.
doesNotProve: That any private Arc-enabled server, storage account, or deployment is configured or working.
That SAS URIs should be logged, shared, or stored.
That Run Command is a high-volume work dispatcher instead of an administrative execution rail.

officialSources.title: Cloudflare Workers Documentation
supports: Official Cloudflare starting points for Workers and related developer documentation.
A source trail for serverless edge application, deployment, and platform-behavior research.
doesNotProve: That a private Cloudflare account, Worker, queue, domain, or deployment is configured a particular way.
That a feature is available in every account, plan, or region without checking the current Cloudflare docs and account context.

officialSources.title: Azure Samples Communication Services Virtual Visits JavaScript
supports: Microsoft's Azure Samples source for a branded Azure Communication Services virtual appointment web app.
A Bookings-centered sample with /book and /visit routes, Microsoft Bookings configuration, Teams staff join, and client join through a hosted ACS web app.
A public source trail for tracking upstream main, release tags, Node/runtime expectations, package versions, and Bookings integration behavior.
doesNotProve: That any private deployment currently runs the observed version or configuration.
That upstream main is safe to auto-deploy without review.
That a separate multisite web app should import the sample's ACS token, meeting, room, or Teams interop logic.

officialSources.title: Azure Samples Communication Services Web Calling Hero
supports: Microsoft's Azure Samples source for a generic group calling experience using @azure/communication-react.
A comparison point for newer ACS calling sample package versions and raw calling UX patterns.
doesNotProve: That the web calling hero sample replaces the Bookings-centered Virtual Visits sample for appointment workflows.
That a private appointment stack should abandon Microsoft Bookings or Teams interoperability.

officialSources.title: Azure Communication UI Library
supports: The project source and documentation for @azure/communication-react and ACS UI components.
A source trail for component, composite, peer-dependency, and package-version drift that can affect ACS virtual appointment samples.
doesNotProve: That the UI library repository is itself a complete appointment deployment.
That beta package metadata should be adopted by a production app without compatibility review.

officialSources.title: Azure Communication Services Virtual Appointment Building Blocks
supports: Official Microsoft documentation for ACS Teams interoperability, Rooms concepts, and access-token issuance.
A source trail for the underlying ACS concepts that virtual appointment samples rely on.
doesNotProve: That a private tenant, Teams policy, ACS resource, room, meeting, or token service is configured correctly.
That a sample deployment satisfies production security, privacy, compliance, or operational requirements.

officialSources.title: Microsoft Graph Bookings API Overview
supports: Official Microsoft Graph starting point for Bookings businesses, services, staff, customers, appointments, and related scheduling objects.
A source trail for deciding whether a workflow needs Bookings iframe configuration or deeper Graph integration.

A public-safe source trail for custom first-party booking pages that use Graph for services, staff availability, customers, and appointment creation while Bookings remains the scheduling system of record.
doesNotProve: That a private Microsoft 365 tenant has the needed permissions or Bookings configuration.
That Graph integration is required when a hosted Bookings page and Virtual Visits sample are sufficient.
That browser-side code should call Microsoft Graph directly.
That application-permission appointment creation enforces Bookings business rules without backend validation.

officialSources.title: GitHub Documentation For Syncing A Fork
supports: Official GitHub guidance for bringing upstream changes into a fork.
A source trail for an upstream-tracking repo pattern where a focused deployment branch reviews Microsoft sample updates before deployment.
doesNotProve: That upstream changes are safe to merge or deploy automatically.
That a fork-sync workflow replaces application-specific build, security, privacy, or regression checks.

officialSources.title: GitHub Documentation For Repository READMEs
supports: Official GitHub guidance that a README explains why a project is useful and how it can be used.
A source trail for public repository documentation and Markdown formatting behavior.
doesNotProve: That a repository is maintained, authoritative, licensed for reuse, or complete by itself.
That a README replaces a source index, manifest, changelog, or official vendor documentation.

officialSources.title: Heffl API v2 Task Assignment And Linking
supports: Official Heffl API v2 documentation for task creation, task listing, contact/deal creation, deal pipeline listing, and user listing.
A source trail for task assignment via assigneeIds and record linking via entity/entityId.
A source trail for confirming Heffl's API v2 production server base and deal-linking prerequisites.
doesNotProve: That any private Heffl account, API key, user, task, lead, deal, project, or permission is configured a particular way.
That task assignment to another user works in a private deployment without live authorization checks.
That this source trail replaces current official documentation.
